  118→        \"\"\"\n   119→        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n   120→\n   121→        # Clean data\n   122→        df = clean_data(df)\n   123→\n   124→        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n   125→        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n   126→\n   127→        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n   128→ 136→\n   137→        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n   138→        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n   139→\n   140→        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n   150→        return df\n   151→\n   152→    def evolve_population(\n   153→        self,\n   154→        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n   155→        y_train: pd.Series,\n   156→        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n   157→    ) -> Population:\n   158→        \"\"\"\n   159→        Evolve a population of models.\n   160→        \"\"\"\n   161→        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n   162→\n   163→        pop = Population(\n   164→            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n   165→            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n   166→            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n   167→            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n   168→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n   169→        )\n   170→\n   171→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n   172→\n   173→        for gen in range(generations):\n   174→            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n   175→\n   176→            # Evaluate all individuals\n   177→            for ind in pop.individuals:\n   178→                try:\n   179→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n   180→                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n   181→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n   182→                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n   183→\n   184→                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n   185→                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n   186→                except Exception as e:\n   187→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   188→\n   189→            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n   190→            if gen < generations - 1:\n   191→                pop.evolve()\n   192→\n   193→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   194→            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   195→\n   196→        return pop\n   197→\n   198→    def run_walk_forward(\n   199→        self,\n   200→        df: pd.DataFrame\n   201→    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n   202→        \"\"\"\n   203→        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n   204→        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n   205→        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n   206→        \"\"\"\n   207→        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n   208→\n   209→        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n   210→\n   211→        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n   212→        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n   213→        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n   214→\n   215→        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n   216→        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n   217→            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n   218→            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n   219→\n   220→        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n   221→        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n   222→        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n   223→\n   224→        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n   225→        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n   226→            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n   227→            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n   228→\n   229→        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n   230→\n   231→        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n   232→        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n   233→        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n   234→        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n   235→\n   236→        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n   237→        pop = Population(\n   238→            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n   239→            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n   240→            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n   241→            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n   242→            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n   243→        )\n   244→        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n   245→\n   246→        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n   247→            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n   248→            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n   249→            for ind in pop.individuals:\n   250→                try:\n   251→                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n   252→                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n   253→                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n   254→                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n   255→                except Exception as e:\n   256→                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   257→                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   258→\n   259→            pop.evolve()\n   260→            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n   261→            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n   262→\n   263→        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n   264→        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n   265→\n   266→        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n   267→        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n   268→        for ind in pop.individuals:\n   269→            # Skip individuals that failed training\n   270→            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n   271→                continue\n   272→            try:\n   273→                # Predict on validation\n   274→                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n   275→                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n   276→                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n   277→                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n   278→                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n   279→                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n   280→                # Run simulation\n   281→                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n   282→                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n   283→                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n   284→                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n   285→                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n   286→                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n   287→            except Exception as e:\n   288→                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n   289→                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n   290→\n   291→        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n   292→        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n   293→\n   294→        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n   295→        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n   296→            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n   297→            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n   298→        )\n   299→        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n   300→\n   301→        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n   302→        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n   303→\n   304→        # Run simulator on signals\n   305→        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n   306→        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n   307→\n   308→        # Compute raw accuracy\n   309→        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n   310→        if mask.any():\n   311→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n   312→        else:\n   313→            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n   314→\n   315→        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n   316→        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n   317→\n   318→        return metrics\n   319→\n   320→    def train_best_models(\n   321→        self,\n   322→        df: pd.DataFrame,\n   323→        population: Population,\n   324→        horizon_data: dict = None\n   325→    ):\n   326→        \"\"\"\n   327→        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n   328→        \"\"\"\n   329→        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n   330→\n   331→        # Use recent models\n   340→        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n   341→\n   342→        # Retrain on full recent data\n   343→        for ind in top_models:\n Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n   441→        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n   442→\n   443→        # 7. Generate current signals\n   444→        if len(current_df) > 0:\n   445→            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n   446→            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n   447→            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\")\n   448→            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n   449→            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\")\n   450→        else:\n   451→            signals = pd.Series()\n   452→            latest_signal = None\n   453→\n   454→        # 8. Save results\n   455→        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n   456→\n   457→        # Compile full result\n   458→        result = {\n   459→            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n   460→            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n   461→            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n   462→            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n   463→            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n   464→            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n   465→            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n   466→            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n   467→        }\n   468→\n   469→        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n   470→        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n   471→        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n   472→        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n   473→\n   474→        return result\n   475→\n   476→    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n   477→        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n   478→        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n   479→        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n   480→        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n   481→\n   482→        # Save top models\n   490→        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n   491→        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n   492→        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n   493→        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n   494→            try:\n   495→                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n   496→            except Exception as e:\n   497→                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\")\n   498→\n   499→        # min_train_samples=ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES\n        )\n\n        self.trader = SimulatedTrader(\n            initial_capital=TradingConfig.INITIAL_CAPITAL,\n            fee_rate=TradingConfig.FEE_RATE,\n            slippage=TradingConfig.SLIPPAGE,\n            bet_size=TradingConfig.POSITION_SIZE\n        )\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble = min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n\n    def load_or_fetch_data(self, force_refetch: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\n            print(f\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\")\n            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\"open_time\"])\n        else:\n            print(\"Fetching fresh data from Binance...\")\n            df = fetch_and_save_historical(\n                symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to   \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n\n        # Merge horizon features into df\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                except Exception as e:\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            pop.evolve()\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for ind in pop.individuals:\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                continue\n            try:\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\")\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\")\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Save top models\n        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\")\n\n        # Save population metadata\n        pop_data = []\n        for ind in population.individuals[:10]:\n            pop_data.append({\n                \"model_type\": ind.model_type,\n                \"params\": ind.params,\n                \"fitness\": float(ind.fitness)\n            })\n        with open(run_dir / \"population.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(pop_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Results saved to {run_dir}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Entry point for running the trading system.\"\"\"\n    system = TradingSystem()\n\n    # str:\n        return f\"{n / 1000:.1f}k\" if n >= 1000 else str(n)\n    tps = \"\"\n    if elapsed > 0 and out > 0:\n        tps = f\" | {out / elapsed:.0f} t/s\"\n    cost = (inp * COST_PER_M_INPUT + out * COST_PER_M_OUTPUT) / 1_000_000\n    cost_str = f\" | ${cost:.4f}\" if cost >= 0.0001 else \"\"\n    return f\"{_k(inp)} in / {_k(out)} out{tps}{cost_str}\"\n\n\n# ── Prompt helpers to chatlog, rolling to a new file when size exceeds limit.\"\"\"\n    with _chatlog_lock:\n        CHATLOG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        max_file_size = 4000\n        max_files = \"\"\"Load recent Telegram chat history.\"\"\"\n    if not CHATLOG_DIR.exists():\n        return \"\"\n    files = sorted(CHATLOG_DIR.glob(\"*.jsonl\"))\n    if not files:\n        return \"\"\n\n    lines: list[str] = []\n    total = 0\n    for f in reversed(files):\n        for raw in reversed(f.read_text().strip().splitlines()):\n            try:\n                msg = json.loads(raw)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError:\n                continue\n            entry = f\"[{msg.get('ts', '?')[:16]}] {msg['role']}: {msg['text']}\"\n            if total + len(entry) > max_chars:\n                lines.reverse()\n                return \"## Recent Telegram chat\\n\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(lines)\n            lines.append(entry)\n            total += len(entry) + 1\n\n    lines.reverse()\n    if not lines:\n        return \"\"\n    return \"## Recent Telegram chat\\n\\n\" + \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\n# ── Step update helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n\ndef \"\"\"Download a file from Telegram and save it to FILES_DIR.\"\"\"\n    FILES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    file_info = bot.get_file(file_id)\n    downloaded = bot.download_file(file_info.file_path)\n    save_path = FILES_DIR / filename\n    # avoid overwriting: append a suffix if the file already exists\n    if save_path.exists():\n        stem, suffix = telegram file: {save_path.name} ({len(downloaded)} bytes)\")\n    return save_path\n\n\n# ── Chutes proxy (Anthropic Messages API → OpenAI Chat Completions) ──────────\n\n_proxy_app = FastAPI(title=\"Chutes Proxy\")\n\n\ndef _convert_tools_to_openai(anthropic_tools: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n    out = []\n    for t in anthropic_tools:\n        out.append({\n            \"type\": \"function\",\n            \"function\": {\n                \"name\": t[\"name\"],\n                \"description\": t.get(\"description\", \"\"),\n                \"parameters\": t.get(\"input_schema\", {\"type\": \"object\", \"properties\": {}}),\n            },\n        })\n    return out\n\n\ndef _convert_messages_to_openai(\n    messages: list[dict], system: str | list | None = None\n) -> list[dict]:\n    out: list[dict] = []\n\n    if system:\n        if isinstance(system, list):\n            text_parts = [b[\"text\"] for b in system if b.get(\"type\") == \"text\"]\n            system = \"\\n\\n\".join(text_parts)\n        if system:\n            out.append({\"role\": \"system\", \"content\": system})\n\n    for msg in messages:\n        role = msg[\"role\"]\n        content = msg.get(\"content\", \"\")\n\n        if isinstance(content, str):\n            out.append({\"role\": role, \"content\": content})\n            continue\n\n        if not isinstance(content, list):\n            out.append({\"role\": role, \"content\": str(content)})\n            continue\n\n        text_parts: list[str] = []\n        tool_calls: list[dict] = []\n        tool_results: list[dict] = []\n        image_parts: list[dict] = []\n\n        for block in content:\n            btype = block.get(\"type\", \"\")\n\n            if btype == \"text\":\n                text_parts.append(block[\"text\"])\n\n            elif btype == \"tool_use\":\n                tool_calls.append({\n                    \"id\": block[\"id\"],\n                    \"type\": \"function\",\n                    \"function\": {\n                        \"name\": block[\"name\"],\n                        \"arguments\": json.dumps(block.get(\"input\", {})),\n                    },\n                })\n\n            elif btype == \"tool_result\":\n                result_content = block.get(\"content\", \"\")\n                if isinstance(result_content, list):\n                    result_content = \"\\n\".join(\n                        b.get(\"text\", \"\") for b in result_content if b.get(\"type\") == \"text\"\n                    )\n                tool_results.append({\n                    \"role\": \"tool\",\n                    \"tool_call_id\": block[\"tool_use_id\"],\n                    \"content\": str(result_content),\n                })\n\n            elif btype == \"image\":\n                source = block.get(\"source\", {})\n                if source.get(\"type\") == \"base64\":\n                    image_parts.append({\n                        \"type\": \"image_url\",\n                        \"image_url\": {\n                            \"url\": f\"data:{source.get('media_type', 'image/png')};base64,{source['data']}\"\n                        },\n                    })\n\n        if role == \"assistant\":\n            oai_msg: dict[str, Any] = {\"role\": \"assistant\"}\n            if text_parts:\n                oai_msg[\"content\"] = \"\\n\".join(text_parts)\n            else:\n                oai_msg[\"content\"] = None\n            if tool_calls:\n                oai_msg[\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n            out.append(oai_msg)\n\n        elif role == \"user\":\n            if tool_results:\n                for tr in tool_results:\n                    out.append(tr)\n            if text_parts or image_parts:\n                if image_parts:\n                    content_blocks = [{\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": t} for t in text_parts] + image_parts\n                    out.append({\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": content_blocks})\n                elif text_parts:\n                    out.append({\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"\\n\".join(text_parts)})\n        else:\n            out.append({\"role\": role, \"content\": \"\\n\".join(text_parts) if text_parts else \"\"})\n\n    return out\n\n\ndef _build_openai_request(body: dict, *, routing: str = \"agent\") -> dict:\n    routing_model = CHUTES_ROUTING_BOT if routing == \"bot\" else CHUTES_ROUTING_AGENT\n    oai: dict[str, Any] = {\n        \"model\": routing_model,\n        \"messages\": _convert_messages_to_openai(\n            body.get(\"messages\", []),\n            system=body.get(\"system\"),\n        ),\n    }\n    if \"max_tokens\" in body:\n        oai[\"max_tokens\"] = body[\"max_tokens\"]\n    if oai[\"temperature\"] = body[\"temperature\"]\n    if body.get(\"top_p\") is not None:\n        oai[\"top_p\"] = body[\"top_p\"]\n    if body.get(\"stream\"):\n        oai[\"stream\"] = True\n        oai[\"stream_options\"] = {\"include_usage\": True}\n    return oai\n\n\ndef _openai_response_to_anthropic(oai_resp: dict, model: str) -> dict:\n    choice = oai_resp.get(\"choices\", [{}])[0]\n    message = choice.get(\"message\", {})\n    finish = choice.get(\"finish_reason\", \"stop\")\n\n    content_blocks: list[dict] = []\n    if message.get(\"content\"):\n        content_blocks.append({\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": message[\"content\"]})\n    for tc in (message.get(\"tool_calls\") or []):\n        try:\n            args = json.loads(tc[\"function\"][\"arguments\"])\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError):\n            args = {}\n        content_blocks.append({\n            \"type\": \"tool_use\",\n            \"id\": tc.get(\"id\", f\"toolu_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}\"),\n            \"name\": tc[\"function\"][\"name\"],\n            \"input\": args,\n        })\n\n    if finish == \"tool_calls\":\n        stop_reason = \"tool_use\"\n    elif finish == \"length\":\n        stop_reason = \"max_tokens\"\n    else:\n        stop_reason = \"end_turn\"\n\n    usage = oai_resp.get(\"usage\", {})\n    return {\n        \"id\": oai_resp.get(\"id\", f\"msg_{uuid.uuid4().hex}\"),\n        \"type\": \"message\",\n        \"role\": \"assistant\",\n        \"model\": model,\n        \"content\": content_blocks 0),\n            \"output_tokens\": usage.get(\"completion_tokens\", 0),\n        },\n    }\n\n\ndef _sse_event(event: str, data: dict) -> str:\n    return f\"event: {event}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n\nasync def _stream_openai_to_anthropic(oai_response: httpx.Response, model: str):\n    msg_id = f\"msg_{uuid.uuid4().hex}\"\n    yield _sse_event(\"message_start\", {\n        \"type\": \"message_start\",\n        \"message\": {\n            \"id\": msg_id, \"type\": \"message\", \"role\": \"assistant\",\n            \"model\": model, \"content\": [], \"stop_reason\": None,\n            \"stop_sequence\": None,\n            \"usage\": {\"input_tokens\": 0, \"output_tokens\": 0},\n        },\n    })\n\n    block_idx = 0\n    in_text_block = False\n    tool_calls_accum: dict[int, dict] = {}\n    stop_reason = \"end_turn\"\n    usage = {\"input_tokens\": 0, \"output_tokens\": 0}\n    logged_stream_model = False\n\n    async for line in oai_response.aiter_lines():\n        if not line.startswith(\"data: \"):\n            continue\n        data_str = line[6:].strip()\n        if data_str == \"[DONE]\":\n            break\n  if not choices:\n            continue\n\n        delta = choices[0].get(\"delta\", {})\n        finish = choices[0].get(\"finish_reason\")\n\n        if finish == \"tool_calls\":\n            stop_reason = \"tool_use\"\n        elif finish == \"length\":\n            stop_reason = \"max_tokens\"\n        elif finish == \"stop\":\n            stop_reason = \"end_turn\"\n\n        if delta.get(\"content\"):\n            if not in_text_block:\n                yield _sse_event(\"content_block_start\", {\n                    \"type\": \"content_block_start\",\n                    \"index\": block_idx,\n                    \"content_block\": {\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"\"},\n                })\n                in_text_block = True\n            yield _sse_event(\"content_block_delta\", {\n                \"type\": \"content_block_delta\",\n                \"index\": block_idx,\n                \"delta\": {\"type\": \"text_delta\", \"text\": tc_idx = tc.get(\"index\", 0)\n                if tc_idx not in tool_calls_accum:\n                    tool_calls_accum[tc_idx] = {\n                        \"id\": tc.get(\"id\", \"block_idx\": block_idx,\n                    }\n                    yield _sse_event(\"content_block_start\", {\n                        \"type\": \"content_block_start\",\n                     \"name\": tool_calls_accum[tc_idx][\"name\"],\n                            \"input\": {},\n                        },\n                    })\n                    block_idx += 1\n                args_chunk = tc.get(\"function\", {}).get(\"arguments\", \"\")\n                if args_chunk:\n                    tool_calls_accum[tc_idx][\"arguments\"] += args_chunk\n                    yield _sse_event(\"content_block_delta\", {\n                        \"type\": \"content_block_delta\",\n                        \"index\": tool_calls_accum[tc_idx][\"block_idx\"],\n                        \"delta\": {\"type\": \"input_json_delta\", \"partial_json\": args_chunk},\n                    })\n\n    with _token_lock:\n        _token_usage[\"input\"] += usage[\"input_tokens\"]\n \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {LLM_API_KEY}\",\n        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    }\n\n\n@_proxy_app.get(\"/health\")\nasync def _proxy_health():\n    return {\"status\": \"ok\"}\n\n\n@_proxy_app.get(\"/\")\nasync def _proxy_root():\n    return {\n        \"proxy\": \"chutes\",\n        \"pool\": CHUTES_POOL,\n        \"agent_routing\": CHUTES_ROUTING_AGENT,\n        \"bot_routing\": CHUTES_ROUTING_BOT,\n        \"status\": \"running\",\n    }\n\n\n_CONTEXT_LENGTH_RE = re.compile(\n    r\"maximum context length is (\\d+) tokens.*?(\\d+) output tokens.*?(\\d+) input tokens\",\n    re.DOTALL,\n)\nPROXY_MAX_RETRIES = 3\n\n\ndef _parse_context_length_error(error_msg: str) -> tuple[int, int, int] | None:\n    \"\"\"Extract (context_limit, requested_output, input_tokens) from a context-length 400.\"\"\"\n    m = _CONTEXT_LENGTH_RE.search(error_msg)\n    if m:\n        return int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2)), int(m.group(3))\n    return None\n\n\ndef _maybe_reduce_max_tokens(oai_request: dict, error_msg: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"If the error is a context-length overflow, reduce max_tokens to fit. Returns True if adjusted.\"\"\"\n    parsed = _parse_context_length_error(error_msg)\n    if not parsed:\n        return False\n    ctx_limit, _req_output, input_tokens = parsed\n    headroom = ctx_limit - input_tokens\n    if headroom < 1024:\n        return False\n    new_max = max(1024, headroom - 64)\n    old_max = oai_request.get(\"max_tokens\", 0)\n    if new_max >= old_max:\n        return False\n    oai_request[\"max_tokens\"] = new_max\n    _log(f\"proxy: reduced max_tokens {old_max} -> {new_max} (ctx_limit={ctx_limit}, input={input_tokens})\")\n    return True\n\n\n@_proxy_app.post(\"/v1/messages\")\nasync def _proxy_messages(request: Request):\n    body = await request.json()\n    stream = body.get(\"stream\", False)\n    model = body.get(\"model\", else CHUTES_ROUTING_AGENT\n\n    if stream:\n        last_error_msg = \"\"\n        for attempt in range(1, PROXY_MAX_RETRIES + 1):\n            try:\n                client =    if resp.status_code == 400 and _maybe_reduce_max_tokens(oai_request, last_error_msg):\n                        continue\n                    if attempt < PROXY_MAX_RETRIES:\n                     \"message\": f\"Chutes routing failed ({resp.status_code}): {last_error_msg[:300]}\",\n                        },\n                    })\n\n                async def generate(resp=resp, cl=client):\n                    try:\n                        _log(f\"proxy: streaming [{routing}] via {routing_label}\")\n                        async for event in            },\n                })\n            except Exception as exc:\n                last_error_msg = str(exc)[:300]\n                _log(f\"proxy: error (attempt {attempt}/{PROXY_MAX_RETRIES}):           try:\n                async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=httpx.Timeout(PROXY_TIMEOUT)) as client:\n                    resp = await client.post(\n on_activity=None):\n    \"\"\"Run a single claude subprocess, return (returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr).\n\n    on_text: optional callback(accumulated_text) fired as assistant text streams in.\n    on_activity: optional callback(status_str) fired on tool use and other activity.\n    Kills the process if no output is received for CLAUDE_TIMEOUT seconds.\n    \"\"\"\n    proc = subprocess.Popen(\n        cmd, cwd=WORKING_DIR, env=env,\n        stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,\n        stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE,\n        text=True, bufsize=1,\n    )\n    with _child_procs_lock:\n        _child_procs.add(proc)\n\n    result_text = \"\"\n    complete_texts: list[str] = []\n    streaming_tokens: list[str] = []\n    raw_lines: list[str] = []\n    timed_out = False\n    last_activity = time.monotonic()\n\n    sel = selectors.DefaultSelector()\n    sel.register(proc.stdout, selectors.EVENT_READ)\n\n    try:\n        while True:\n            ready = sel.select(timeout=min(CLAUDE_TIMEOUT, 30))\n            if not ready:\n                if time.monotonic() - last_activity > CLAUDE_TIMEOUT:\n                    _log(f\"claude timeout: no output for {CLAUDE_TIMEOUT}s, killing pid={proc.pid}\")\n                    proc.kill()\n                    timed_out = True\n                    break\n                if proc.poll() is not None:\n                    break\n                continue\n            line = proc.stdout.readline()\n            if not line:\n                break\n            last_activity = time.monotonic()\n            raw_lines.append(line)\n            try:\n                evt = json.loads(line)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError:\n                continue\n            etype = evt.get(\"type\", \"\")\n            if etype == \"assistant\":\n                msg = evt.get(\"message\", {})\n                for block in msg.get(\"content\", []):\n                    btype = block.get(\"type\", \"\")\n                    if btype == \"text\" and block.get(\"text\"):\n                        if evt.get(\"model_call_id\"):\n                            complete_texts.append(block[\"text\"])\n                            streaming_tokens.clear()\n                        else:\n                            streaming_tokens.append(block[\"text\"])\n                            if on_text:\n                                on_text(\"\".join(streaming_tokens))\n                    elif btype == \"tool_use\" and on_activity:\n                        tool_name = block.get(\"name\", \"\")\n     _token_usage[\"output\"] += u.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n            elif etype == \"item.completed\":\n                item = evt.get(\"item\", {})\n                if item.get(\"type\") == evt.get(\"usage\", {})\n                    if u:\n                        with _token_lock:\n                            _token_usage[\"input\"] += u.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n                            _token_usage[\"output\"] += u.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n    finally:\n        sel.unregister(proc.stdout)\n        sel.close()\n\n    if not result_text:\n        if complete_texts:\n            result_text = complete_texts[-1]\n        elif streaming_tokens:\n            result_text = \"\".join(streaming_tokens)\n\n    if timed_out:\n        stderr_output = \"(timed out)\"\n    else:\n raw_lines, stderr_output\n\n\ndef run_agent(cmd: list[str], phase: str, output_file: Path,\n              on_text=None, on_activity=None, goal_index: int = 0) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:\n    _claude_semaphore.acquire()\n    try:\n        env = _claude_env(goal_index=goal_index)\n        flags = \" \".join(a for a in cmd if a.startswith(\"-\"))\n\n        returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr_output = 1, \"\", [], \"no attempts made\"\n\n        for attempt in range(1, MAX_RETRIES + 1):\n            _log(f\"{phase}: starting (attempt={attempt}) flags=[{flags}]\")\n            t0 = time.monotonic()\n\n            returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr_output = _run_claude_once(\n                cmd, env, on_text=on_text, on_activity=on_activity,\n            )\n            elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0\n\n            output_file.write_text(_redact_secrets(\"\".join(raw_lines)))\n            _log(f\"{phase}: finished rc={returncode} {fmt_duration(elapsed)}\")\n\n            if returncode != 0 and stderr_output.strip():\n                _log(f\"{phase}: stderr {stderr_output.strip()[:300]}\")\n                if attempt < MAX_RETRIES:\n                    delay = min(2 ** attempt, 30)\n                    _log(f\"{phase}: retrying in {delay}s (attempt {attempt}/{MAX_RETRIES})\")\n                    time.sleep(delay)\n                    continue\n\n            return subprocess.CompletedProcess(\n                args=cmd, returncode=returncode,\n                stdout=result_text, stderr=stderr_output,\n            )\n\n        _log(f\"{phase}: all {MAX_RETRIES} retries exhausted\")\n        output_file.write_text(_redact_secrets(\"\".join(raw_lines)))\n        return subprocess.CompletedProcess(\n            args=cmd, returncode=returncode,\n            stdout=result_text, stderr=stderr_output,\n        )\n    finally:\n        _claude_semaphore.release()\n\n\ndef extract_text(result: subprocess.CompletedProcess) -> str:\n    output = result.stdout or \"\"\n    if not output.strip():\n        output = result.stderr or \"(no output)\"\n    return output\n\n\ndef run_step(prompt:     _log(f\"step message finalize failed: {str(exc)[:120]}\")\n\n\n# ── Agent loop ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n\ndef _goal_loop(index: int):\n    \"\"\"Run the agent loop for a single goal. Exits when stop_event is set.\"\"\"\n    global _step_count\n\n    with _goals_lock:\n        gs = _goals.get(index)\n    if not gs:\n        return\n\n    failures = 0\n    gf = _goal_file(index)\n\n    while not gs.stop_event.is_set():\n        if not gf.exists() or not gf.read_text().strip():\n            if gs.goal_hash:\n                _log(f\"goal #{index} cleared after {gs.step_count} steps\")\n                gs.goal_hash = \"\"\n                gs.step_count = 0\n            gs.wake.wait(timeout=5)\n            gs.wake.clear()\n            continue\n\n        if gs.paused:\n            gs.wake.wait(timeout=5)\n            gs.wake.clear()\n            continue\n\n        current_goal = gf.read_text().strip()\n        current_hash = _step_count += 1\n        gs.step_count += 1\n        gs.last_run = datetime.now().isoformat()\n        with _goals_lock:\n            _save_goals()\n\n        _log(f\"Goal #{index} Step {gs.step_count} (global step {_step_count})\", blank=True)\n\n        prompt = load_prompt(goal_index=index, consume_inbox=True, goal_step=gs.step_count)\n        if not prompt:\n \"0\"))\n        if failures:\n            backoff = min(2 ** failures, 120)\n            step_delay += backoff\n            _log(f\"goal #{index}: waiting {step_delay}s (failure backoff + delay)\")\n _log(f\"goal #{index} loop exited\")\n\n\ndef _goal_manager():\n    \"\"\"Monitor _goals and spawn/stop goal threads as needed.\"\"\"\n    while not _shutdown.is_set():\n        with _goals_lock:\n _recent_context(max_chars: int = 6000) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Collect recent rollouts across all goals.\"\"\"\n    parts: list[str] = []\n    total = 0\n    all_runs: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []\n    for _build_operator_prompt(user_text: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Build prompt for the CLI agent to handle any operator request.\"\"\"\n    chatlog = load_chatlog(max_chars=4000)\n\n    parts = [\n        \"You are the operator interface for Arbos, a coding agent running in a loop via pm2.\\n\"\n        \"The operator communicates with you through Telegram. Be concise and direct.\\n\"\n        \"When the operator asks you to do something, do it by modifying the relevant files.\\n\"\n        \"When the operator asks a question, answer from the available context.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Security\\n\\n\"\n        \"NEVER read, output, or reveal the contents of `.env`, `.env.enc`, or any secret/key/token values.\\n\"\n        \"Do not include API keys, passwords, seed phrases, or credentials in any response.\\n\"\n        \"If asked to show secrets, refuse. The .env file is encrypted; do not attempt to decrypt it.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Multi-goal system\\n\\n\"\n        \"Goals are indexed and stored in `context/goals/<index>/`. Each goal has its own GOAL.md, STATE.md, INBOX.md, and runs/.\\n\"\n        \"Goal management is handled via Telegram commands (/goal, /start, /stop, /pause, /delete, /delay, /ls, /status).\\n\"\n        \"To modify a specific goal's context, write to `context/goals/<index>/STATE.md` or `context/goals/<index>/INBOX.md`.\\n\\n\"\n        \"## Available operations\\n\\n\"\n        \"- **Message a goal's agent**: append a timestamped line to `context/goals/<index>/INBOX.md`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Update a goal's state**: write to `context/goals/<index>/STATE.md`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Set system prompt**: write to `PROMPT.md`.\\n\"\n        \"- **Set env variable**: write `KEY='VALUE'` lines (one per line) to `context/.env.pending`. They are picked up automatically and persisted.\\n\"\n        \"- **View logs**: read files in `context/goals/<index>/runs/<timestamp>/` (rollout.md, logs.txt).\\n\"\n        \"- **Modify code & \"Bash\":\n        detail = (tool_input.get(\"command\") or \"\")[:80]\n    elif tool_name in (\"Read\", \"Write\", \"Edit\"):\n        detail = (tool_input.get(\"file_path\") or        detail = (tool_input.get(\"url\") or \"\")[:60]\n    elif tool_name == \"WebSearch\":\n        detail = (tool_input.get(\"query\") or tool_input.get(\"search_term\") or \"\")[:60]\n    elif run_agent_streaming(bot, prompt: str, chat_id: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Run Claude Code CLI and stream output into a Telegram message.\"\"\"\n    if PROVIDER == \"openrouter\":\n        cmd = _claude_cmd(prompt)\n    else:\n        cmd = _claude_cmd(prompt, extra_flags=[\"--model\", \"bot\"])\n\n    msg = bot.send_message(chat_id, \"thinking...\")\n    current_text = \"\"\n    activity_status = \"\"\n    last_edit = 0.0\n\n    def _edit(text: str, force: bool = False):\n        nonlocal last_edit\n        now = time.time()\n        if not force and now - last_edit < 1.5:\n            return\n        display = text[-3800:] if len(text) > 3800 else text\n        display = _redact_secrets(display)\n        if not display.strip():\n            return\n        try:\n            bot.edit_message_text(display, chat_id, msg.message_id)\n            last_edit = now\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n\n    def _on_text(text: str):\n        nonlocal current_text\n        current_text = text\n        _edit(text)\n\n    def _on_activity(status: str):\n        nonlocal activity_status\n        activity_status = status\n        if not current_text:\n            _edit(status)\n\n    _claude_semaphore.acquire()\n    try:\n        env = _claude_env()\n\n        for attempt in range(1, MAX_RETRIES + 1):\n            current_text = \"\"\n            activity_status = \"\"\n            last_edit = 0.0\n\n            returncode, result_text, raw_lines, stderr_output = _run_claude_once(\n                cmd, env, on_text=_on_text, on_activity=_on_activity,\n            )\n\n            if result_text.strip():\n                current_text = result_text\n                break\n\n            if returncode != 0 and attempt < MAX_RETRIES:\n                delay = min(2 ** attempt, 30)\n                _edit(f\"Error, retrying in {delay}s... (attempt {attempt}/{MAX_RETRIES})\", force=True)\n                time.sleep(delay)\n                continue\n            break\n\n        _edit(current_text, force=True)\n\n        if not _claude_semaphore.release()\n\n    return current_text\n\n\ndef _is_owner(user_id: int) -> bool:\n    owner = os.environ.get(\"TELEGRAM_OWNER_ID\", \"\").strip()\n    if not owner:\n        return      bot.send_message(message.chat.id, \"No goals. @bot.message_handler(commands=[\"clear\"])\n    def handle_clear(message):\n        uid = message.from_user.id if message.from_user else None\n        if not _is_owner(uid):\n ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ndef _kill_child_procs():\n    \"\"\"Kill all tracked claude child processes.\"\"\"\n    with _child_procs_lock:\n        procs = list(_child_procs)\n    for proc in procs:\n        try:\n            if proc.poll() is None:\n                _log(f\"killing child claude pid={proc.pid}\")\n                proc.kill()\n                proc.wait(timeout=5)\n        except Exception:\n            pass\n    with _child_procs_lock:\n        _child_procs.clear()\n\n\ndef _kill_stale_claude_procs():\n    \"\"\"Kill any leftover claude processes from a previous arbos instance.\"\"\"\n    my_pid = os.getpid()\n    try:\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            [\"pgrep\", \"-x\", \"claude\"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5,\n        )\n        for line in result.stdout.strip().splitlines():\n            pid = int(line.strip())\n            if pid == my_pid:\n                continue\n PermissionError:\n                pass\n    except Exception:\n        pass\n\n\ndef _send_cli(args: list[str]):\n    \"\"\"CLI entry point: python arbos.py send 'message' [--file path]\n\n    Within a step, all sends are consolidated into a single Telegram message.\n    The first send creates it; subsequent sends edit it by appending.\n    Uses ARBOS_GOAL_INDEX env var to find the per-goal step message file.\n    \"\"\"\n    import argparse\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Send a Telegram message to the operator\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"message\", nargs=\"?\", help=\"Message text to send\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--file\", help=\"Send contents of a file instead\")\n    parsed = parser.parse_args(args)\n\n    if not parsed.message and not parsed.file:\n        parser.error(\"Provide a message or --file\")\n\n    if parsed.file:\n        text = Path(parsed.file).read_text()\n    else:\n        text = parsed.message\n\n    goal_index = log_chat(\"bot\", combined[:1000])\n            print(f\"Edited step message ({len(combined)} chars)\")\n        else:\n            new_id = _send_telegram_new(text)\n            if new_id:\n                smf.write_text(json.dumps({\"msg_id\": new_id, \"text\": text}))\n                log_chat(\"bot\", text[:1000])\n                print(f\"Sent new message ({len(text)} chars)\")\n **Controlled drawdown**\\n\\n- **Robustness across market regimes**\\n\\n- **Stable out-of-sample performance**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Fitness Priorities\\n\\nFitness should prioritize:\\n\\n1. **Directional accuracy on 15m BTC prediction**\\n\\n2. **Monthly bet count**\\n\\n3. **PnL**\\n\\n4. **Sharpe ratio**\\n\\n5. **Drawdown control**\\n\\n6. **Regime robustness**\\n\\n7. **Consistency across walk-forward folds**\\n\\nAny candidate that fails either:\\n\\n- **accuracy < 65%**\\n\\n- **bets/month <= 90**\\n\\nshould be considered below target even if other metrics look good.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Data and Modeling Requirements\\n\\nThe system should:\\n\\n- learn from **BTC market structure**\\n\\n- exploit **short-term directional patterns**\\n\\n- use **multi-timescale context** while keeping the target fixed at 15m\\n\\n- adapt online as conditions change\\n\\n- discover alpha from:\\n\\n  - price action\\n\\n  - volatility\\n\\n  - momentum\\n\\n  - order-flow proxies\\n\\n  - volume\\n\\n  - derivatives context if available\\n\\n  - regime features\\n\\n  - cross-horizon agreement\\n\\nLonger horizons {1h, 4h, 8h, 12h, 24h} should be used as **contextual ensemble inputs**, not as replacements for the core 15m target.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Validation Rules\\n\\nThe system must use:\\n\\n- strict **temporal splits**\\n\\n- strict **walk-forward validation**\\n\\n- no lookahead bias\\n\\n- no leakage\\n\\n- no random split for final evaluation\\n\\n- evaluation on realistic trading frequency\\n\\n- explicit measurement of:\\n\\n  - accuracy\\n\\n  - number of bets\\n\\n  - PnL\\n\\n  - Sharpe\\n\\n  - drawdown\\n\\n  - regime-specific performance\\n\\nA candidate is only truly good if it remains strong in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Evolution Strategy\\n\\nRun this loop continuously:\\n\\nloop t = 1..∞\\n\\n    S_t = design_or_modify(S_{t-1})   # implement the current design\\n\\n    O_t = run(S_t)                    # train, validate, and trade / paper trade\\n\\n    P_t = measure(O_t)                # evaluate accuracy, bets/month, Sharpe, PnL, drawdown, regime behavior\\n\\n    Δ_t = reflect(S_t, P_t)           # identify weaknesses and bottlenecks\\n\\n    S_{t+1} = improve(S_t, Δ_t)       # produce a better design\\n\\nend\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Reflection Requirements\\n\\nDuring reflection, explicitly diagnose:\\n\\n- why accuracy is below 65% if target is missed\\n\\n- why signal frequency is below 90 bets/month if target is missed\\n\\n- whether the consensus gate is too strict\\n\\n- whether the model is overfitting to specific regimes\\n\\n- whether the ensemble is improving or diluting edge\\n\\n- whether the system is sacrificing too much frequency for precision\\n\\n- whether the system is producing too many low-quality trades\\n\\n- whether thresholding should be tightened or loosened\\n\\nThe next design must directly address the identified weakness.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Design Guidance\\n\\nThe system should search for the best balance between:\\n\\n- **high directional precision**\\n\\n- **high enough trade frequency**\\n\\n- **strong risk-adjusted return**\\n\\n- **robustness through time**\\n\\nThis is not a low-frequency swing system.  \\n\\nThis is not a generic forecasting system.  \\n\\nIt is a **BTC 15-minute directional trading system** that must be both:\\n\\n- **accurate enough** (>= 65%)\\n\\n- **active enough** (> 90 bets/month)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## True Success Condition\\n\\nThe process should continue until the {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"gen-1773804936-K3KwSQePoUYNpqLnyyA2","type":"message","role":"assistant","container":null,"content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I need to find the bottleneck. The horizon feature generation seems okay. But I notice that in `train_best_models`, there's a section that trains horizon ensemble:\n\n```python\nfor h_name, factor in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.items():\n    if h_name == \"15m\":\n        continue\n    from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n    df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n```\n\nBut `resample_to_horizon` expects `horizon_minutes` and `factor`. The call is: `resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)` - that passes `recent_days` as the second argument, which is wrong! That's a bug. It should be the period in minutes, not days.\n\nWait, looking at the signature: `resample_to_horizon(df: pd.DataFrame, horizon_minutes: int, factor: int)`. It expects horizon_minutes (like 60 for 1h) and factor (like 4). But they're passing `recent_days` (an integer like 60) as the horizon_minutes, and `factor` as factor. That would try to resample to `recent_days` minutes (like 60 minutes? That might be okay for 1h, but if recent_days=60, then 60 minutes = 1 hour, that happens to be okay for 1h horizon, but for 4h (240 minutes) they'd still pass 60, which is wrong). Actually they're inside a loop over horizons, so for each h_name they should compute the horizon_minutes from the name or from a mapping. But they're using a wrong function call.\n\nActually let's look at the call site:\n\n```python\nfor h_name, factor in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.items():\n    if h_name == \"15m\":\n        continue\n    # Resample and prepare features for this horizon\n    from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n    df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n    ...\n```\n\nThe second argument is `recent_days` (which is 60). That's clearly wrong - should be something like the horizon in minutes. There's also a separate `factor` which is the resampling factor relative to 15m. The function `resample_to_horizon` uses the `horizon_minutes` to resample via pandas' resample method with a frequency string like `\"{horizon_minutes}T\"`. So passing 60 would mean 60 minutes = 1 hour. That works for 1h horizon, but for 4h it would be 240 minutes. So this is a bug: it's using the same `recent_days` (60) for all horizons, meaning it would resample everything to 1-hour bars, not to 4h, 8h, etc. That would cause wrong features.\n\nBut that's not necessarily causing the timeout. However, it might cause issues if the resampling frequency is mismatched with factor.\n\nThe bigger issue: why no output for an hour? Let's check where the system might be blocking.\n\nIn run_online_step:\n1. load_or_fetch_data: This loads data from CSV or fetches from Binance. Should be fast.\n2. prepare_features: This generates horizon features, which for each horizon calls add_technical_indicators and add_regime_features. Those functions might compute rolling windows that could be expensive if done many times. But horizon generation loops over 6 horizons (15m,1h,4h,8h,12h,24h). For each, it resamples and recomputes technical indicators. For a 90-day dataset of 15m bars (~8640 rows), after resampling to 24h, you'd have 90 rows. That's fine. That's 2.5 minutes. With 3 generations, that's 7.5 minutes. Still far from 60 minutes. But if each fit takes 10 minutes, then it would time out.\n\nWhy would model.fit take 10 minutes? {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"gen-1773804958-ROzuoYKMNVlNLXJzcxBJ","type":"message","role":"assistant","container":null,"content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Technical indicators look standard and should be fast.\n\nLet me think deeper about the bottleneck. The log says \"claude timeout: no output for 3600s\". The arbos wrapper monitors stdout from the claude process and if no output for 3600s, it kills it. That means for 60 minutes, the claude subprocess (which is executing the step) didn't write anything to stdout that the wrapper detected.\n\nInside the claude process, the step code runs `system.run_online_step()`. That function prints many things with flush=True. But if it hangs in a function call that doesn't print (e.g., model.fit, or some pandas operation), then no output will be seen.\n\nGiven that earlier they did a medium test that completed in ~8 minutes and got results, but now a full run with similar parameters (pop=10, gens=3, max_train=15000) seems to hang, maybe there's a difference: The online step does both walk-forward validation AND then trains best models on full data AND generates current signals. The function signature expects `(df, horizon_minutes: int, factor: int)`. They are passing `recent_days` (an integer like 60) as the horizon_minutes. That means for all horizons they are resampling to the same frequency: 60-minute bars. That might actually reduce the data size more than intended, but also leads to incorrect horizon features.\n\nBut the bigger issue: The `resample_to_horizon` function uses pandas resample: `resample(f\"{horizon_minutes}T\")`. If they pass 60, it's 60T = 60 minutes = 1 hour. That's okay for 1h horizon but wrong for others. But the factor argument is also being passed (e.g., for 4h, factor=16) but used? In the function, the `factor` argument is not used at all! The function uses only `horizon_minutes` for resampling and ignores the factor. That's a bug: the function doesn't need factor if it resamples by time. It seems they intended to use factor to do manual grouping like in horizon.py's `resample_df`. Indeed, there are two different resample functions: one in horizon.py (`resample_df` using groupby) and one in processor.py (`resample_to_horizon` using pandas resample). The train_best_models uses the processor version incorrectly, passing factor that is ignored. The horizon generation uses horizon.resample_df which is correct.\n\nThe horizon ensemble training likely takes the recent_df (last 60 days of 15m data) and for each horizon (1h,4h,8h,12h,24h) it resamples to that horizon's bar size. But because they pass 60 as horizon_minutes, they effectively resample everything to 60-minute bars, regardless of the factor. So for 4h they'd still get 60m data, which is inconsistent. But would that cause a hang? Maybe if the resampled data ends up with many rows (60 days of 60m data = 60*24 = 1440 rows) still manageable. But they also compute technical indicators and regime features on each, so that's some extra work but not huge.\n\nThe bigger hang could be in `generate_horizon_features` which is called in `prepare_features`. Should be okay.\n\nMaybe the real issue: The `run_walk_forward` method calls `self.validator`? Actually they inlined the walk-forward; no separate validator class.\n\nWait: In `run_online_step`, after feature preparation, they call `self.evolve_population` and then `self.run_walk_forward`. The run_walk_forward method itself creates a fresh population and evolves it again on the training split (which is limited). But note that evolve_population earlier was called on the online training data (train_df, which is data up to 30 days ago). That's one evolution. Then run_walk_forward does its own evolution on a different split (df_wf). So two evolutions per step! That could double work. But still not 60 minutes.\n\nMaybe the problem is in the population management: `pop.evolve()` might be doing something that becomes slow as population evolves, like deep copying individuals with large models? But the models are sklearn objects, not huge.\n\nAnother candidate: In `run_walk_forward`, after evolving the population, they re-score each individual on the validation set. initial_capital) / initial_capital\n\n    metrics = {\n        \"accuracy\": accuracy,\n        \"bets_per_month\": bets_per_month,\n        \"sharpe\": sharpe,\n        \"max_drawdown\": abs(max_drawdown),\n        \"total_return\": total_return,\n        \"profit_factor\": profit_factor,\n        \"n_trades\": len(trades),\n        \"final_equity\": capital,\n        \"gross_profit\": gross_profit,\n        \"gross_loss\": gross_loss\n    }\n\n    return metrics\n\n\ndef evaluate_predictions(\n    predictions: pd.Series,\n    actuals: pd.Series,\n    signals: pd.Series = None\n) -> Dict[str, float]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Evaluate directional accuracy and betting performance.\n    \"\"\"\n    preds = predictions.reindex(actuals.index)\n    mask = preds.notna() & actuals.notna()\n\n    if not mask.any():\n        return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets\": 0}\n\n    aligned_preds = preds[mask]\n    aligned_actuals = actuals[mask]\n\n    correct = (aligned_preds == aligned_actuals).sum()\n    accuracy = correct / len(aligned_preds)\n\n    # Count bets (non -1 signals)\n    if signals is not None:\n        bets = (signals.reindex(actuals.index) != -1).sum()\n    else:\n        bets = len(aligned_preds)\n\n    return {\n        \"accuracy\": accuracy,\n        \"bets\": bets,\n        \"n_samples\": len(aligned_preds)\n    }\n","numLines":149,"startLine":1,"totalLines":149}}}

Combined transaction cost: fee + slippage\n        entry_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n        exit_cost = self.fee_rate + self.slippage\n\n        # Align signals and prices\n        signals = signals.reindex(prices.index).fillna(-1)\n\n        # Build equity curve indexed by signals.index\n        equity = pd.Series(index=signals.index, dtype=float)\n        equity.iloc[0] = capital\n\n        # Iterate through each timestamp, use signal to open a trade that closes at next timestamp\n        for i in range(len(signals) - 1):\n            sig = signals.iloc[i]\n            current_price = prices.iloc[i]\n            next_price = prices.iloc[i+1]\n\n            if sig == -1:\n                # No trade, carry forward capital\n                equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n                continue\n\n            if sig == 1:  # long\n                entry = current_price * (1 + entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 - exit_cost)\n                ret = (exit / entry) - 1\n            else:  # sig == 0, short\n                entry = current_price * (1 - entry_cost)\n                exit = next_price * (1 + exit_cost)\n                ret = (entry / exit) - 1\n\n            # Apply bet size\n            capital *= (1 + ret * self.bet_size)\n\n            self.trades.append({\n                \"entry_time\": signals.index[i],\n                \"exit_time\": signals.index[i+1],\n                \"entry_price\": entry,\n                \"exit_price\": exit,\n                \"return\": ret,\n                \"pnl\": capital - self.initial_capital\n            })\n\n            equity.iloc[i+1] = capital\n\n        # Fill any remaining NaNs in equity with last value\n        equity = equity.ffill()\n\n        self.equity_curve = equity\n\n        # Calculate metrics\n        self.metrics = self._compute_metrics()\n\n        return self.metrics\n\n    def _compute_metrics(self) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"Compute metrics from simulation.\"\"\"\n        if not self.trades:\n            return {\n                \"accuracy\": 0.0,\n                \"bets_per_month\": 0.0,\n                \"sharpe\": 0.0,\n                \"max_drawdown\": 0.0,\n                \"total_return\": 0.0,\n                \"n_trades\": 0,\n                \"profit_factor\": 0.0,\n                \"final_equity\": self.initial_capital\n            }\n\n        returns = [t[\"return\"] for t in self.trades]\n        winning = sum(1 for r in returns if r > 0)\n        accuracy = winning / len(returns)\n\n        # Bets per month (entries)\n        n_entries = len(self.trades)\n        if self.equity_curve is not None and len(self.equity_curve) > 0:\n            period_days = len(self.equity_curve) / (24 * 4)  # 15-min candles to days\n        else:\n            period_days = 0\n        months = period_days / 30.44\n        bets_per_month = n_entries / months if months > 0 else 0\n\n        # Sharpe\n        if self.equity_curve is not None:\n            eq_returns = self.equity_curve.pct_change().dropna()\n            if eq_returns.std() > 0:\n                sharpe = (eq_returns.mean() / eq_returns.std()) * np.sqrt(365 * 24 * 4)\n            else:\n                sharpe = 0.0\n\n            # Max drawdown\n            running_max = self.equity_curve.expanding().max()\n            drawdown = (self.equity_curve - running_max) / running_max\n            max_drawdown = abs(drawdown.min())\n        else:\n            sharpe = 0.0\n            max_drawdown = 0.0\n\n        total_return = (self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] - self.initial_capital) / self.initial_capital\n\n        gross_profit = sum(r for r in returns if r > 0)\n        gross_loss = abs(sum(r for r in returns if r < 0))\n        profit_factor = gross_profit / (gross_loss + 1e-10)\n\n        return {\n            \"accuracy\": accuracy,\n            \"bets_per_month\": bets_per_month,\n            \"sharpe\": sharpe,\n            \"max_drawdown\": max_drawdown,\n            \"total_return\": total_return,\n            \"n_trades\": len(self.trades),\n            \"profit_factor\": profit_factor,\n            \"final_equity\": self.equity_curve.iloc[-1] if self.equity_curve is not None else self.initial_capital,\n            \"gross_profit\": gross_profit,\n            \"gross_loss\": gross_loss\n        }\n\n    def get_equity_curve(self) -> pd.Series:\n        return self.equity_curve\n\n    def get_trades(self) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        return pd.DataFrame(self.trades)\n","numLines":174,"startLine":1,"totalLines":174}}}

np.random.choice([3, 5, 7])\n            return {\"n_estimators\": int(n_est), \"learning_rate\": float(lr), \"max_depth\": int(max_depth)}\n        elif model_type == \"simple_nn\":\n            hidden_sizes = random.choice(ModelConfig.SIMPLE_NN_HIDDEN_SIZE)\n            layers = random.choice(ModelConfig.SIMPLE_NN_LAYERS)\n            # Build a multi-layer structure\n            sizes = [int(hidden_sizes)] * layers\n            return {\n                \"hidden_sizes\": sizes,\n                \"dropout\": np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.5),\n                \"learning_rate\": np.random.uniform(0.0001, 0.001),\n                \"epochs\": 30\n            }\n        return {}\n\n    def evaluate_fitness(self, individual: Individual, metrics: Dict[str, float]):\n        \"\"\"\n        Compute composite fitness score.\n\n        Fitness = w1*accuracy + w2*bet_ratio + w3*sharpe - w4*drawdown\n        where bet_ratio = (bets_per_month / target_bets) capped at 1\n        \"\"\"\n        accuracy = metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0)\n        bets_per_month = metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0)\n        sharpe = metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0)\n        drawdown = metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0)\n\n        # Normalize bet count (encourage reaching 90)\n        target_bets = 90.0\n        bet_ratio = min(bets_per_month / target_bets, 1.0)\n\n        # Dual penalty for missing targets\n        accuracy_penalty = 0.0 if accuracy >= 0.65 else (0.65 - accuracy) * 10\n        bet_penalty = 0.0 if bets_per_month >= 90 else (90 - bets_per_month) / 90.0 * 5\n\n        # Weighted sum (tunable)\n        fitness = (\n            2.0 * accuracy +\n            1.0 * bet_ratio +\n            0.5 * max(sharpe, 0) -\n            0.5 * drawdown\n        ) - accuracy_penalty - bet_penalty\n\n        individual.fitness = fitness\n        individual.metrics = metrics.copy()\n\n    def _select_parents(self) -> Tuple[Individual, Individual]:\n        \"\"\"Tournament selection.\"\"\"\n        tournament_size = 3\n\n        def tournament():\n            contestants = random.sample(self.individuals, tournament_size)\n            return max(contestants, key=lambda ind: ind.fitness)\n\n        return tournament(), tournament()\n\n    def _mutate(self, individual: Individual) -> Individual:\n        \"\"\"Apply random mutations to hyperparameters.\"\"\"\n        if random.random() > self.mutation_rate:\n            return individual\n\n        new_params = individual.params.copy()\n        model_type = individual.model_type\n\n        if model_type == \"logistic\":\n            if random.random() < 0.5:\n                new_params[\"C\"] = float(np.random.choice([0.01, 0.1, 1.0, 10.0, 100.0]))\n        elif model_type == \"random_forest\":\n            if random.random() < 0.5:","numLines":150,"startLine":1,"totalLines":252}}}
