)\n        except ImportError:\n            from sklearn.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier\n            self.model = GradientBoostingClassifier(\n                n_estimators=n_estimators,\n                learning_rate=learning_rate,\n                max_depth=max_depth,\n                random_state=random_state\n            )\n        self._params = {\n            \"n_estimators\": n_estimators,\n            \"learning_rate\": learning_rate,\n            \"max_depth\": max_depth,\n            \"random_state\": random_state\n        }\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def fit(self, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series) -> \"GradientBoostingModel\":\n        self.model.fit(X, y)\n        self.is_fitted = True\n        return self\n\n    def predict_proba(self, X: pd.DataFrame) -> np.ndarray:\n        if not self.is_fitted:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Model must be fitted first\")\n        return self.model.predict_proba(X)[:, 1]\n\n    def predict(self, X: pd.DataFrame, threshold: float = 0.5) -> np.ndarray:\n        proba = self.predict_proba(X)\n        return (proba >= threshold).astype(int)\n\n    def get_params(self) -> dict:\n        return self.model.get_params()\n\n    def set_params(self, params: dict):\n        self.model.set_params(**params)\n        self._params.update(params)\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n\nclass SimpleNNModel(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"Simple feedforward neural network using PyTorch or sklearn MLP.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        input_dim: int,\n        hidden_sizes: list = [64, 32],\n        dropout: float = 0.2,\n        learning_rate: float = 0.001,\n        epochs: int = 50,\n        batch_size: int = 32,\n        random_state: int = 42\n    ):\n        self.input_dim = input_dim\n        self.hidden_sizes = hidden_sizes\n        self.dropout = dropout\n        self.learning_rate = learning_rate\n        self.epochs = epochs\n        self.batch_size = batch_size\n        self.random_state = random_state\n\n        self._params = {\n            \"input_dim\": input_dim,\n            \"hidden_sizes\": hidden_sizes,\n            \"dropout\": dropout,\n            \"learning_rate\": learning_rate,\n            \"epochs\": epochs,\n            \"batch_size\": batch_size,\n            \"random_state\": random_state\n        }\n\n        self.model = None\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def _build_model(self):\n        \"\"\"Build the neural network.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            import torch\n            import torch.nn as nn\n\n            class Net(nn.Module):\n                def __init__(self, input_dim, hidden_sizes, dropout):\n                    super().__init__()\n                    layers = []\n                    prev_dim = input_dim\n                    for h in hidden_sizes:\n                        layers.append(nn.Linear(prev_dim, h))\n                        layers.append(nn.ReLU())\n                        layers.append(nn.Dropout(dropout))\n                        prev_dim = h\n                    layers.append(nn.Linear(prev_dim, 1))\n                    self.net = nn.Sequential(*layers)\n\n                def forward(self, x):\n                    return torch.sigmoid(self.net(x))\n\n            self.model = Net(self.input_dim, self.hidden_sizes, self.dropout)\n            self.optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(self.model.parameters(), lr=self.learning_rate)\n            self.criterion = nn.BCELoss()\n            self.use_torch = True\n        except ImportError:\n            from sklearn.neural_network import MLPClassifier\n            self.model = MLPClassifier(\n                hidden_layer_sizes=self.hidden_sizes,\n                activation=\"relu\",\n                solver=\"adam\",\n                alpha=self.dropout,  # L2 penalty as proxy for dropout\n                learning_rate_init=self.learning_rate,\n                max_iter=self.epochs,\n                batch_size=self.batch_size,\n                random_state=self.random_state,\n                verbose=False\n            )\n            self.use_torch = False\n            self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def fit(self, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series) -> \"SimpleNNModel\":\n        if self.model is None:\n            self._build_model()\n\n        X_arr = X.values.astype(np.float32)\n        y_arr = y.values.astype(np.float32)\n\n        if self.use_torch:\n            import torch\n            from torch.utils.data import TensorDataset, DataLoader\n\n            X_tensor = torch.FloatTensor(X_arr)\n            y_tensor = torch.FloatTensor(y_arr).unsqueeze(1)\n            dataset = TensorDataset(X_tensor, y_tensor)\n            loader = DataLoader(dataset, batch_size=self.batch_size, shuffle=True)\n\n            self.model.train()\n            for epoch in range(self.epochs):\n                for X_batch, y_batch in loader:\n                    self.optimizer.zero_grad()\n                    preds = self.model(X_batch)\n                    loss = self.criterion(preds, y_batch)\n                    loss.backward()\n                    self.optimizer.step()\n        else:\n            self.model.fit(X, y)\n\n        self.is_fitted = True\n        return self\n\n    def predict_proba(self, X: pd.DataFrame) -> np.ndarray:\n        if not self.is_fitted:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Model must be fitted first\")\n\n        X_arr = X.values.astype(np.float32)\n\n        if self.use_torch:\n            import torch\n            self.model.eval()\n            with torch.no_grad():\n                X_tensor = torch.FloatTensor(X_arr)\n                probs = self.model(X_tensor).numpy().flatten()\n            return probs\n        else:\n            return self.model.predict_proba(X)[:, 1]\n\n    def predict(self, X: pd.DataFrame, threshold: float = 0.5) -> np.ndarray:\n        proba = self.predict_proba(X)\n        return (proba >= threshold).astype(int)\n\n    def get_params(self) -> dict:\n        return self._params.copy()\n\n    def set_params(self, params: dict):\n        self._params.update(params)\n        # Rebuild model if architecture params change\n        hidden_changed = \"hidden_sizes\" in params and params[\"hidden_sizes\"] != self.hidden_sizes\n        input_changed = \"input_dim\" in params and params[\"input_dim\"] != self.input_dim\n        if hidden_changed or input_changed:\n            self.model = None\n            self.is_fitted = False\n        for k, v in params.items():\n            setattr(self, k, v)\n        # For sklearn, set_params also updates the sklearn model\n        if not self.use_torch and self.model is not None:\n            self.model.set_params(**params)\n\n\n# horizon -> population\n        self.main_features = []  # list of feature names produced by this ensemble\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def fit_horizon_models(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        target_series: pd.Series\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train a separate model population for each horizon.\n\n        Args:\n            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features for that horizon\n            target_series: target series (same for all, aligned to base timeframe)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.horizon_models = {}\n\n        for horizon, df in horizon_data.items():\n            if df.empty:\n                continue\n\n            # Align target to this horizon's timestamps\n            df_indexed = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n            target_aligned = target_series.reindex(df_indexed.index)\n\n            # Prepare features (exclude time and target columns)\n            feature_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in [\"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]]\n            X = df[feature_cols]\n\n            # Create population (use only random_forest for speed and stability)\n            pop = Population(\n                population_size=5,  # small for horizon models\n                model_types=[\"random_forest\"],\n                elitism_count=1\n            )\n            pop.initialize(input_dim=len(feature_cols), feature_names=feature_cols)\n\n            # Quick training: just fit all models (simplified, should be evolutionary)\n            self._train_population(pop, X, target_aligned)\n            self.horizon_models[horizon] = pop\n\n        self.is_fitted = True\n\n    def _train_population(self, population: Population, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Fit all models in a population.\"\"\"\n        for ind in population.individuals:\n            try:\n                ind.model.fit(X, y)\n            except Exception as e:\n                # Set very low fitness if model fails\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n    def generate_features(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        base_index: pd.DatetimeIndex\n    ) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate horizon ensemble features: predictions from each horizon's best model.\n\n        Returns:\n            DataFrame with columns like: h_1h_pred, h_1h_proba, h_4h_pred, ...\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.is_fitted:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"HorizonEnsemble must be fitted first\")\n\n        features = pd.DataFrame(index=base_index)\n\n        for horizon, pop in self.horizon_models.items():\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n\n            # Get horizon data aligned to base index\n            df_h = horizon_data[horizon].set_index(\"open_time\")\n            X_h = df_h.reindex(base_index)\n\n            # Drop non-feature columns\n            feature_cols = [c for c in X_h.columns if c not in [\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\", \"quote_volume\"]]\n            X_h_features = X_h[feature_cols]\n\n            # Forward fill any missing features (from resampling gaps)\n            X_h_features = X_h_features.ffill()\n\n            # Generate predictions\n            try:\n                proba = best.model.predict_proba(X_h_features)\n                pred = best.model.predict(X_h_features, threshold=0.5)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = proba\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = pred\n            except Exception as e:\n                # If prediction fails, fill with 0.5 (neutral)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = 0.5\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = 0\n\n        self.main_features = list(features.columns)\n        return features\n\n    def get_feature_names(self) -> List[str]:\n        return self.main_features\n","numLines":121,"startLine":1,"totalLines":121}}}

But if the tool itself (a subprocess) produces no output for a while, the Claude process might not write anything. For example, if the Python script runs without printing, the tool's output might be buffered, and the Claude process only gets the output when the tool finishes or flushes. So if the Python script runs for an hour without flushing stdout, the tool's output pipe might not deliver anything until the tool exits or the buffer fills (usually 64KB). If the script prints a lot, it would fill the pipe and block, but if it prints little, the buffer might not fill for a long time. That means if generations=1, they train the initial population, then evolve to produce a new generation. So they end with a second generation of offspring. That's extra training? Their model is created (via create_model) but not fitted. But they catch exceptions and set fitness = -1000. So offspring likely fail and get -1000, and are skipped. The elites are the ones that were trained in the previous generation. So effectively only elites are rescored. import SimulatedTrader\nfrom .trading.metrics import evaluate_predictions\n\n\nclass TradingSystem:\n    \"\"\"\n    Main trading system class that orchestrates the full workflow.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        data_path: str = None,\n        results_dir: str = PathConfig.RESULTS_DIR,\n        log_dir: str = PathConfig.LOGS_DIR\n    ):\n        self.data_path = data_path self.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        self.log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Current state\n        self.df = None\n        self.feature_names = []\n        self.main_population = None\n        self.horizon_ensemble = None\n        self.consensus_gate = None\n        self.validator = None\n        self.trader = None\n\n        # Load or initialize\n        self._init_components()\n\n    def _init_components(self):\n        \"\"\"Initialize all system components.\"\"\"\n        self.validator = WalkForwardValidator(\n            train_days=ValidationConfig.TRAIN_DAYS,\n            valid_days=ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS,\n            step_days=ValidationConfig.STEP_DAYS,\n            min_train_samples=ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES\n        )\n\n        self.trader = SimulatedTrader(\n            initial_capital=TradingConfig.INITIAL_CAPITAL,\n            fee_rate=TradingConfig.FEE_RATE,\n            slippage=TradingConfig.SLIPPAGE,\n            bet_size=TradingConfig.POSITION_SIZE\n        )\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble = HorizonEnsemble(horizons=FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n        self.consensus_gate = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n\n    def load_or_fetch_data(self, force_refetch: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\n            print(f\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\")\n            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\"open_time\"])\n        else:\n            print(\"Fetching fresh data from Binance...\")\n            df = fetch_and_save_historical(\n                symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n\n        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n        print(\"  Generating horizon features...\", flush=True)\n        horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n\n        # Merge horizon features into df\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\",\n                \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"future_close\", \"target\"\n            ]\n        ]\n\n        print(f\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def evolve_population(\n        self,\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n        y_train: pd.Series,\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                except Exception as e:\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            pop.evolve()\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for ind in pop.individuals:\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                continue\n            try:\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\n        recent_days = 60\n        cutoff_time = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\n        recent_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\n\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_recent = recent_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Get top models\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n\n        # Retrain on full recent data\n        for ind in top_models:\n            try:\n                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided\n        if horizon_data is None:\n            print(\"Training horizon ensemble...\", flush=True)\n            horizon_data = {}\n            for h_name, factor in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.items():\n                if h_name == \"15m\":\n                    continue\n                # Resample and prepare features for this horizon\n                from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n                df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n                if len(df_horizon) > 100:\n                    df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\n                    df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\n                    df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\n                    df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\n                    horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n\n        self.main_population = population\n        self.top_models = top_models\n\n    def generate_current_signals(\n        self,\n        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n        use_horizon: bool = True\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n\n        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n        return signals\n\n    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n\n        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\")\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\")\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Save metrics\n        with open(run_dir / \"metrics.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)\n\n        # Save signals\n        signals.to_csv(run_dir / \"signals.csv\")\n\n        # Save top models\n        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\")\n\n        # Save population metadata\n        pop_data = []\n        for ind in population.individuals[:10]:\n            pop_data.append({\n                \"model_type\": ind.model_type,\n                \"params\": ind.params,\n                \"fitness\": float(ind.fitness)\n            })\n        with open(run_dir / \"population.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(pop_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Results saved to {run_dir}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Entry point for running the trading system.\"\"\"\n    system = TradingSystem()\n\n    # Fetch latest data\n2. Generate features (including horizon ensembles)\n3. Evolve model population\n4. Train best models with walk-forward validation\n5. Generate signals via consensus gating\n6. Paper trade and measure performance\n7. Reflect on metrics and adjust system\n8. Repeat\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\nimport sys\nimport json\nimport pickle\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Dict, Generator, Tuple, Optional\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\n\nfrom .config import *\nfrom .data.fetcher import BinanceDataFetcher, fetch_and_save_historical\nfrom .features.horizon import generate_horizon_features\nfrom .features.technical import add_technical_indicators\nfrom .features.regime import add_regime_features\nfrom .data.processor import clean_data, create_target\nfrom .models.population import Population, Individual\nfrom .ensemble.horizon_ensemble import HorizonEnsemble\nfrom .ensemble.consensus import ConsensusGate\nfrom .validation.walk_forward import WalkForwardValidator\nfrom .trading.simulator import SimulatedTrader\nfrom .trading.metrics import evaluate_predictions\n\n\nclass TradingSystem:\n    \"\"\"\n    Main trading system class that orchestrates the full workflow.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        data_path: str = None,\n        results_dir: str = PathConfig.RESULTS_DIR,\n        log_dir: str = PathConfig.LOGS_DIR\n    ):\n        self.data_path = data_path or Path(PathConfig.DATA_DIR) / f\"{DataConfig.SYMBOL.lower()}_{DataConfig.INTERVAL}.csv\"\n        self.results_dir = Path(results_dir)\n        self.log_dir = Path(log_dir)\n\n        self.results_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n        self.log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Current state\n        self.df = None\n        self.feature_names = []\n        self.main_population = None\n        self.horizon_ensemble = None\n        self.consensus_gate = None\n        self.validator = None\n        self.trader = None\n\n        # Load or initialize\n        self._init_components()\n\n    def _init_components(self):\n        \"\"\"Initialize all system components.\"\"\"\n        self.validator = WalkForwardValidator(\n            train_days=ValidationConfig.TRAIN_DAYS,\n            valid_days=ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS,\n            step_days=ValidationConfig.STEP_DAYS,\n            min_train_samples=ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES\n        )\n\n        self.trader = SimulatedTrader(\n            initial_capital=TradingConfig.INITIAL_CAPITAL,\n            fee_rate=TradingConfig.FEE_RATE,\n            slippage=TradingConfig.SLIPPAGE,\n            bet_size=TradingConfig.POSITION_SIZE\n        )\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble = HorizonEnsemble(horizons=FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n        self.consensus_gate = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n\n    def load_or_fetch_data(self, force_refetch: bool = False) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load data from disk or fetch from Binance if not available/forced.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not force_refetch and self.data_path.exists():\n            print(f\"Loading data from {self.data_path}\")\n            df = pd.read_csv(self.data_path, parse_dates=[\"open_time\"])\n        else:\n            print(\"Fetching fresh data from Binance...\")\n            df = fetch_and_save_historical(\n                symbol=DataConfig.SYMBOL,\n                interval=DataConfig.INTERVAL,\n                start_date=DataConfig.START_DATE,\n                filepath=str(self.data_path)\n            )\n\n        if df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"No data available\")\n\n        print(f\"Loaded {len(df)} rows of data\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Date range: {df['open_time'].iloc[0]} to {df['open_time'].iloc[-1]}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def prepare_features(self, df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Prepare feature matrix X and target y.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Preparing features...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Clean data\n        df = clean_data(df)\n\n        # Create target (next 15-minute direction)\n        df = create_target(df, forward_periods=1)\n\n        # Generate horizon ensemble features (these will be added as extra columns)\n        print(\"  Generating horizon features...\", flush=True)\n        horizon_features = generate_horizon_features(df, FeatureConfig.HORIZONS)\n\n        # Merge horizon features into df\n        df = df.merge(horizon_features, on=\"open_time\", how=\"left\")\n\n        # Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\",\n                \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"future_close\", \"target\"\n            ]\n        ]\n\n        print(f\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def evolve_population(\n        self,\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n        y_train: pd.Series,\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n                try:\n                    print(f\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            pop.evolve()\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for ind in pop.individuals:\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                continue\n            try:\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\n        recent_days = 60\n        cutoff_time = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\n        recent_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\n\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_recent = recent_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Get top models\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n\n        # Retrain on full recent data\n        for ind in top_models:\n            try:\n                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided\n        if horizon_data is None:\n            print(\"Training horizon ensemble...\", flush=True)\n            horizon_data = {}\n            for h_name, factor in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.items():\n                if h_name == \"15m\":\n                    continue\n                # Resample and prepare features for this horizon\n                from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n                df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n                if len(df_horizon) > 100:\n                    df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\n                    df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\n                    df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\n                    df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\n                    horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n\n        self.main_population = population\n        self.top_models = top_models\n\n    def generate_current_signals(\n        self,\n        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n        use_horizon: bool = True\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n\n        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n        return signals\n\n    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n\n        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\")\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\")\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # Save metrics\n        with open(run_dir / \"metrics.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(metrics, f, indent=2)\n\n        # Save signals\n        signals.to_csv(run_dir / \"signals.csv\")\n\n        # Save top models\n        top_models = population.get_best(5)\n        model_dir = run_dir / \"models\"\n        model_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(top_models):\n            try:\n                ind.model.save(str(model_dir / f\"model_{i}_{ind.model_type}.pkl\"))\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Could not save model {i}: {e}\")\n\n        # Save population metadata\n        pop_data = []\n        for ind in population.individuals[:10]:\n            pop_data.append({\n                \"model_type\": ind.model_type,\n                \"params\": ind.params,\n                \"fitness\": float(ind.fitness)\n            })\n        with open(run_dir / \"population.json\", \"w\") as f:\n            json.dump(pop_data, f, indent=2)\n\n        print(f\"Results saved to {run_dir}\", flush=True)\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Entry point for running the trading system.\"\"\"\n    system = TradingSystem()\n\n    # Run one step\n    result = system.run_online_step()\n\n    if result:\n        print(f\"\\nStep completed. Latest signal: {result['latest_signal']}\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Accuracy: {result['accuracy']:.4f}, Bets/month: {result['bets_per_month']:.2f}\", flush=True)\n    else:\n        print(\"Step did not complete successfully.\", flush=True)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":269,"oldLines":11,"newStart":269,"newLines":13,"lines":[" ","         # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)","         print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)","-        for ind in pop.individuals:","+        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):","             # Skip individuals that failed training","             if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:","+                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)","                 continue","             try:","+                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)","                 # Predict on validation","                 pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)","                 # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index"]},{"oldStart":286,"oldLines":6,"newStart":288,"newLines":7,"lines":["                 v_metrics = self.trader.metrics","                 # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)","                 ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)","+                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)","                 # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed","                 ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics","             except Exception as e:"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

# Drop rows with NaN in target\n        df = df.dropna(subset=[\"target\"])\n\n        # Forward fill any remaining NaNs\n        df = df.ffill().dropna()\n\n        print(f\"Prepared {len(df)} samples with {len(df.columns)} columns\", flush=True)\n        self.feature_names = [\n            col for col in df.columns\n            if col not in [\n                \"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\",\n                \"quote_volume\", \"trades_count\", \"future_close\", \"target\"\n            ]\n        ]\n\n        print(f\"Feature count: {len(self.feature_names)}\", flush=True)\n        return df\n\n    def evolve_population(\n        self,\n        X_train: pd.DataFrame,\n        y_train: pd.Series,\n        generations: int = ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION\n    ) -> Population:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evolve a population of models.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(f\"Evolving population (input_dim={X_train.shape[1]})...\", flush=True)\n\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(generations):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen + 1}/{generations}\", flush=True)\n\n            # Evaluate all individuals\n            for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n                try:\n                    print(f\"    Training individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    proba = ind.model.predict_proba(X_train)\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    accuracy = (pred == y_train).mean()\n\n                    # Simple fitness based on training accuracy (we'll validate on walk-forward later)\n                    ind.fitness = accuracy\n                    print(f\"      -> fitness={ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"      -> training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            # Evolve to next generation, but not after the final generation\n            if gen < generations - 1:\n                print(\"  Evolving to next generation...\", flush=True)\n                pop.evolve()\n\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} (model={best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        return pop\n\n    def run_walk_forward(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame\n    ) -> Dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run walk-forward validation with a single temporal split.\n        Train on data before the validation period, test on the most recent validation period.\n        This provides an honest OOS performance estimate without leakage.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Running walk-forward validation (single split)...\", flush=True)\n\n        df_sorted = df.sort_values(\"open_time\").reset_index(drop=True)\n\n        # Determine validation period length (e.g., 7 days)\n        val_days = ValidationConfig.VALID_DAYS\n        val_rows = int(val_days * 24 * 60 / 15)  # number of 15-min candles\n\n        total_rows = len(df_sorted)\n        if total_rows < val_rows + 1000:\n            print(\"Insufficient data for walk-forward split.\", flush=True)\n            return {\"accuracy\": 0.0, \"bets_per_month\": 0.0, \"n_folds\": 0}\n\n        # Split: validation = most recent val_rows, training = older data\n        valid_df = df_sorted.iloc[-val_rows:].copy()\n        train_df = df_sorted.iloc[:-val_rows].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to most recent MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES (controls memory/time)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        print(f\"Train: {len(train_df)} samples, Validation: {len(valid_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n        X_valid = valid_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_valid = valid_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Evolve a fresh population on the training set\n        pop = Population(\n            population_size=ModelConfig.POPULATION_SIZE,\n            model_types=ModelConfig.MODEL_TYPES,\n            mutation_rate=ModelConfig.MUTATION_RATE,\n            crossover_rate=ModelConfig.CROSSOVER_RATE,\n            elitism_count=ModelConfig.ELITISM_COUNT\n        )\n        pop.initialize(input_dim=X_train.shape[1], feature_names=self.feature_names)\n\n        for gen in range(ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION):\n            print(f\"  Generation {gen+1}/{ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION}\", flush=True)\n            # Evaluate individuals on training data\n            for ind in pop.individuals:\n                try:\n                    ind.model.fit(X_train, y_train)\n                    # Use training accuracy as fitness for selection\n                    pred = ind.model.predict(X_train, threshold=0.5)\n                    ind.fitness = (pred == y_train).mean()\n                except Exception as e:\n                    print(f\"    Training error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                    ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n            pop.evolve()\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n            print(f\"    Best fitness: {best.fitness:.4f} ({best.model_type})\", flush=True)\n\n        # Prepare close prices for simulation (used later and here)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Re-score individuals on validation set using Sharpe (profitability)\n        print(\"Re-scoring population on validation set (Sharpe-based)...\", flush=True)\n        for i, ind in enumerate(pop.individuals):\n            # Skip individuals that failed training\n            if ind.fitness is None or ind.fitness <= -1000:\n                print(f\"  Skipping individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} (failed training)\", flush=True)\n                continue\n            try:\n                print(f\"  Rescoring individual {i+1}/{len(pop.individuals)} ({ind.model_type})\", flush=True)\n                # Predict on validation\n                pred_valid = ind.model.predict(X_valid, threshold=0.5)\n                # Ensure pred_valid is a Series with index\n                if isinstance(pred_valid, np.ndarray):\n                    pred_valid = pd.Series(pred_valid, index=X_valid.index)\n                # Create signals series aligned to close_prices (fill missing with -1)\n                signals_ind = pred_valid.reindex(close_prices.index).fillna(-1).astype(int)\n                # Run simulation\n                self.trader.run(signals_ind, close_prices)\n                v_metrics = self.trader.metrics\n                # Use Sharpe as fitness (higher is better)\n                ind.fitness = v_metrics.get('sharpe', -1000)\n                print(f\"    -> Sharpe: {ind.fitness:.4f}\", flush=True)\n                # Preserve validation metrics for later analysis if needed\n                ind.validation_metrics = v_metrics\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Validation rescoring error: {e}\", flush=True)\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n        # Get top diverse models based on validation Sharpe\n        top_models = pop.get_diverse_best(n=5)\n\n        # Generate consensus signals on validation set\n        consensus = ConsensusGate(\n            min_models_agree=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE,\n            min_confidence=ConsensusConfig.MIN_CONFIDENCE\n        )\n        signals = consensus.decide(top_models, X_valid)\n\n        # Align close prices (indices correspond)\n        close_prices = valid_df[\"close\"]\n\n        # Run simulator on signals\n        self.trader.run(signals, close_prices)\n        metrics = self.trader.metrics.copy()\n\n        # Compute raw accuracy\n        mask = (signals != -1) & y_valid.notna()\n        if mask.any():\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = (signals[mask] == y_valid[mask]).mean()\n        else:\n            metrics[\"accuracy\"] = 0.0\n\n        metrics[\"n_folds\"] = 1\n        print(f\"Walk-forward results: accuracy={metrics['accuracy']:.4f}, bets/month={metrics.get('bets_per_month', 0):.2f}, Sharpe={metrics.get('sharpe', 0):.4f}\", flush=True)\n\n        return metrics\n\n    def train_best_models(\n        self,\n        df: pd.DataFrame,\n        population: Population,\n        horizon_data: dict = None\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train the best models on full recent data and optionally train horizon ensembles.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"Training best models on full data...\", flush=True)\n\n        # Use recent data (last N days) for final training\n        recent_days = 60\n        cutoff_time = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=recent_days)\n        recent_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff_time].copy()\n\n        X_recent = recent_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_recent = recent_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # Get top models\n        top_models = population.get_diverse_best(n=ConsensusConfig.MIN_MODELS_AGREE + 2)\n\n        # Retrain on full recent data\n        for ind in top_models:\n            try:\n                ind.model.fit(X_recent, y_recent)\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"  Failed to train {ind.model_type}: {e}\", flush=True)\n\n        # Train horizon ensemble if not provided\n        if horizon_data is None:\n            print(\"Training horizon ensemble...\", flush=True)\n            horizon_data = {}\n            for h_name, factor in FeatureConfig.HORIZONS.items():\n                if h_name == \"15m\":\n                    continue\n                # Resample and prepare features for this horizon\n                from .data.processor import resample_to_horizon\n                df_horizon = resample_to_horizon(recent_df, recent_days, factor)\n                if len(df_horizon) > 100:\n                    df_horizon = create_target(df_horizon, forward_periods=1)\n                    df_horizon = add_technical_indicators(df_horizon)\n                    df_horizon = add_regime_features(df_horizon)\n                    df_horizon = df_horizon.dropna()\n                    horizon_data[h_name] = df_horizon\n\n        self.horizon_ensemble.fit_horizon_models(horizon_data, y_recent)\n\n        self.main_population = population\n        self.top_models = top_models\n\n    def generate_current_signals(\n        self,\n        current_features: pd.DataFrame,\n        use_horizon: bool = True\n    ) -> pd.Series:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate trading signals for the most recent data points.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.top_models is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Models not trained. Call train_best_models first.\")\n\n        signals = self.consensus_gate.decide(self.top_models, current_features)\n        return signals\n\n    def run_online_step(self, new_data: pd.DataFrame = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute one iteration of the online evolution loop.\n\n        This is the main step method called repeatedly by Arbos.\n        \"\"\"\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"RUNNING ONLINE STEP\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        # 1. Load/fetch latest data\n        if new_data is None:\n            df = self.load_or_fetch_data()\n        else:\n            df = new_data\n\n        # 2. For online operation, limit to most recent N days to bound runtime\n        if ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS is not None:\n            cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS)\n            df = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Online mode: limited data to last {ModelConfig.ONLINE_DATA_DAYS} days ({len(df)} rows)\", flush=True)\n\n        # 3. Prepare features\n        df = self.prepare_features(df)\n\n        # 3. Split into recent data for training and current for prediction\n        # Use last 30 days for training, current for signal generation\n        recent_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=30)\n        train_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] <= recent_cutoff].copy()\n        current_df = df[df[\"open_time\"] > recent_cutoff].copy()\n\n        # Limit training samples to control memory/time (use most recent samples)\n        if len(train_df) > ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            train_df = train_df.tail(ModelConfig.MAX_TRAIN_SAMPLES).copy()\n            print(f\"Limited training to {len(train_df)} most recent samples\", flush=True)\n\n        if len(train_df) < ValidationConfig.MIN_TRAIN_SAMPLES:\n            print(f\"Insufficient training data: {len(train_df)} samples\", flush=True)\n            return None\n\n        X_train = train_df[self.feature_names]\n        y_train = train_df[\"target\"]\n\n        # 4. Evolve population\n        population = self.evolve_population(X_train, y_train, generations=ModelConfig.GENERATIONS_PER_ITERATION)\n\n        # 5. Walk-forward validation on historical folds (use recent data to limit folds)\n        if ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS is not None:\n            wf_cutoff = df[\"open_time\"].max() - timedelta(days=ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS)\n            df_wf = df[df[\"open_time\"] >= wf_cutoff].copy()\n            print(f\"Using {len(df_wf)} recent samples for walk-forward validation (max {ValidationConfig.MAX_WALK_FORWARD_DAYS} days)\", flush=True)\n        else:\n            df_wf = df\n        metrics = self.run_walk_forward(df_wf)\n\n        # 6. Train best models on full recent data and prepare horizon ensemble\n        self.train_best_models(df, population)\n\n        # 7. Generate current signals\n        if len(current_df) > 0:\n            current_features = current_df[self.feature_names]\n            signals = self.generate_current_signals(current_features)\n            print(f\"Generated {len(signals)} current signals\")\n            latest_signal = signals.iloc[-1] if len(signals) > 0 else None\n            print(f\"Latest signal: {latest_signal} (1=UP, 0=DOWN, -1=NO TRADE)\")\n        else:\n            signals = pd.Series()\n            latest_signal = None\n\n        # 8. Save results\n        self._save_results(metrics, population, signals)\n\n        # Compile full result\n        result = {\n            \"timestamp\": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n            \"accuracy\": metrics.get(\"accuracy\", 0.0),\n            \"bets_per_month\": metrics.get(\"bets_per_month\", 0.0),\n            \"sharpe\": metrics.get(\"sharpe\", 0.0),\n            \"max_drawdown\": metrics.get(\"max_drawdown\", 0.0),\n            \"total_return\": metrics.get(\"total_return\", 0.0),\n            \"latest_signal\": int(latest_signal) if latest_signal is not None else None,\n            \"population_fitness\": {str(i): ind.fitness for i, ind in enumerate(population.get_best(5))}\n        }\n\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n        print(\"STEP COMPLETE\", flush=True)\n        print(f\"Metrics: {json.dumps(result, indent=2)}\", flush=True)\n        print(\"=\" * 60, flush=True)\n\n        return result\n\n    def _save_results(self, metrics: dict, population: Population, signals: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Save results to disk.\"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().strftime(\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\")\n        run_dir = self.results_dir / timestamp\n        run_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        # horizon -> population\n        self.main_features = []  # list of feature names produced by this ensemble\n        self.is_fitted = False\n\n    def fit_horizon_models(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        target_series: pd.Series\n    ):\n        \"\"\"\n        Train a separate model population for each horizon.\n\n        Args:\n            horizon_data: dict of {horizon: DataFrame} with features for that horizon\n            target_series: target series (same for all, aligned to base timeframe)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.horizon_models = {}\n\n        for horizon, df in horizon_data.items():\n            if df.empty:\n                continue\n\n            # Align target to this horizon's timestamps\n            df_indexed = df.set_index(\"open_time\")\n            target_aligned = target_series.reindex(df_indexed.index)\n\n            # Prepare features (exclude time and target columns)\n            feature_cols = [c for c in df.columns if c not in [\"open_time\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"]]\n            X = df[feature_cols]\n\n            # Create population (use only random_forest for speed and stability)\n            pop = Population(\n                population_size=5,  # small for horizon models\n                model_types=[\"random_forest\"],\n                elitism_count=1\n            )\n            pop.initialize(input_dim=len(feature_cols), feature_names=feature_cols)\n\n            # Quick training: just fit all models (simplified, should be evolutionary)\n            self._train_population(pop, X, target_aligned)\n            self.horizon_models[horizon] = pop\n\n        self.is_fitted = True\n\n    def _train_population(self, population: Population, X: pd.DataFrame, y: pd.Series):\n        \"\"\"Fit all models in a population.\"\"\"\n        for ind in population.individuals:\n            try:\n                ind.model.fit(X, y)\n            except Exception as e:\n                # Set very low fitness if model fails\n                ind.fitness = -1000.0\n\n    def generate_features(\n        self,\n        horizon_data: Dict[str, pd.DataFrame],\n        base_index: pd.DatetimeIndex\n    ) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate horizon ensemble features: predictions from each horizon's best model.\n\n        Returns:\n            DataFrame with columns like: h_1h_pred, h_1h_proba, h_4h_pred, ...\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.is_fitted:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"HorizonEnsemble must be fitted first\")\n\n        features = pd.DataFrame(index=base_index)\n\n        for horizon, pop in self.horizon_models.items():\n            best = pop.get_best(1)[0]\n\n            # Get horizon data aligned to base index\n            df_h = horizon_data[horizon].set_index(\"open_time\")\n            X_h = df_h.reindex(base_index)\n\n            # Drop non-feature columns\n            feature_cols = [c for c in X_h.columns if c not in [\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\", \"quote_volume\"]]\n            X_h_features = X_h[feature_cols]\n\n            # Forward fill any missing features (from resampling gaps)\n            X_h_features = X_h_features.ffill()\n\n            # Generate predictions\n            try:\n                proba = best.model.predict_proba(X_h_features)\n                pred = best.model.predict(X_h_features, threshold=0.5)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = proba\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = pred\n            except Exception as e:\n                # If prediction fails, fill with 0.5 (neutral)\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_proba\"] = 0.5\n                features[f\"h_{horizon}_pred\"] = 0\n\n        self.main_features = list(features.columns)\n        return features\n\n    def get_feature_names(self) -> List[str]:\n        return self.main_features\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":37,"oldLines":10,"newStart":37,"newLines":14,"lines":["         \"\"\"","         self.horizon_models = {}"," ","-        for horizon, df in horizon_data.items():","+        horizons = list(horizon_data.items())","+        print(f\"Fitting horizon ensembles for {len(horizons)} horizons\", flush=True)","+        for idx, (horizon, df) in enumerate(horizons):","             if df.empty:","+                print(f\"  Horizon {horizon}: empty, skipping\", flush=True)","                 continue"," ","+            print(f\"  Horizon {horizon} ({idx+1}/{len(horizons)}): processing {len(df)} rows\", flush=True)","             # Then train_df includes all rows with open_time <= start, which might be only the first row if timestamps are strictly after start. Usually the data is inclusive.
